question: How many paid sick days do full-time employees get each year?
ground_truth: Full-time employees receive 7 paid sick days (56 hours) per calendar year, available on the first day of employment.

question: How much paid parental leave does Kai Components offer?
ground_truth: 12 weeks of paid parental leave at 100% of base pay, after 12 months of continuous service.

question: How many PTO days does a full-time associate accrue in their first two years?
ground_truth: 15 days (120 hours) per year for 0 to 2 years of service.

question: How much unused PTO can I carry over into next year?
ground_truth: A maximum of 40 hours of unused PTO may carry over; anything above 40 hours on December 31 is forfeited.

question: How much bereavement leave is provided for an immediate family member?
ground_truth: Up to 5 paid days of bereavement leave for an immediate family member (2 paid days for an extended family member).

question: What percentage of the medical premium does the company pay for employee-only coverage?
ground_truth: Kai Components pays 80% of the medical premium for employee-only coverage, and 60% for covered dependents.

question: What is the 401k match and vesting schedule?
ground_truth: 100% match up to 4% of eligible pay, vesting on a three-year cliff schedule.

question: When do full-time employees become eligible for medical benefits?
ground_truth: On the first day of the month following 30 days of employment, for employees scheduled 30+ hours per week.

question: How much does the company contribute to the HSA on the high-deductible plan?
ground_truth: An annual company HSA contribution of $600 for individual coverage and $1,200 for family coverage.

question: How is sales commission calculated and when is it paid?
ground_truth: 4% of net component and electronics sales above the monthly quota (6% on accessories and cables), paid on the 15th of the following month.

question: What is the standard monthly sales quota for a full-time associate?
ground_truth: $18,000 in net sales; commission is paid only on net sales above this quota.

question: What happens to my commission if a customer returns an item?
ground_truth: If merchandise is returned within 30 days, commission paid on that sale is clawed back from the next commission payment.

question: Is there a bonus for selling solar or marine electronics?
ground_truth: Yes — a $25 SPIFF for each solar power kit and each marine electronics unit sold, on top of standard commission and not subject to the quota.

question: How many no-call no-shows lead to termination?
ground_truth: Three no-call, no-show occurrences within a rolling 12-month period result in termination.

question: How quickly must associates greet customers?
ground_truth: Every customer must be greeted within 30 seconds of entering the store.

question: How should static-sensitive components be handled on the floor?
ground_truth: At an ESD-safe workstation using a grounded wrist strap; mishandled static-sensitive stock must be quarantined and reported, not resold.

question: How often are employees paid and on what schedule?
ground_truth: Biweekly, every other Friday; if payday is a bank holiday, pay is deposited the preceding business day.

question: When do non-exempt employees earn overtime pay?
ground_truth: Overtime at 1.5x the regular rate for hours worked over 40 in a workweek (Sunday to Saturday), approved in advance.

question: How far in advance are work schedules posted?
ground_truth: Schedules are posted at least 14 days in advance; the minimum shift length is 4 hours.

question: What is the employee discount on merchandise?
ground_truth: 40% off regular-price merchandise and 20% off sale or clearance merchandise, up to $2,000 in retail value per year.

question: How much tuition reimbursement is available per year?
ground_truth: Up to $3,000 per year for job-related coursework completed with a grade of C or better.

question: What is the employee referral bonus?
ground_truth: $500 for a referral who is hired and remains employed for 90 days, with no annual limit.

question: Do employees get any free products?
ground_truth: Yes — one free maker kit per calendar quarter, up to a retail value of $150, from current-season inventory.

question: What is the SPIFF for solar power kits and marine electronics units?
ground_truth: A $25 SPIFF for each solar power kit and each marine electronics unit sold, in addition to standard commission and not subject to the monthly quota.

question: What are the ESD handling rules for static-sensitive components?
ground_truth: Bare boards, ICs, and static-sensitive components must be handled at an ESD-safe workstation with a grounded wrist strap; damaged stock must be quarantined and reported, not returned to sellable inventory.

question: What is the company HSA contribution for the high-deductible plan?
ground_truth: An annual company HSA contribution of $600 for individual and $1,200 for family coverage.

question: How is overtime calculated for non-exempt employees?
ground_truth: 1.5 times the regular rate for hours worked over 40 in a Sunday-through-Saturday workweek, and all overtime must be approved in advance by a manager.
